title: Bat Removal & Exclusion in Greenburgh, NY | $900 – $2,500
metaDescription: Professional bat removal and exclusion services in Greenburgh, Westchester. Humane, licensed bat control for homes and businesses. Call Best Pest Control NYC today!
intro: Greenburgh homeowners and property managers trust Best Pest Control NYC for safe, humane bat removal and exclusion services. Our certified technicians specialize in identifying bat entry points, removing colonies, and implementing long-term exclusion solutions to keep your property bat-free. With pricing ranging from $900 to $2,500, we offer comprehensive bat control tailored to your Greenburgh property's unique needs.
whyUs: Best Pest Control NYC brings over a decade of experience serving Greenburgh and Westchester County with state-licensed, humane bat removal services. Our team uses proven exclusion techniques that comply with New York wildlife regulations, ensuring bats are removed safely while protecting your home from future infestations.
localContext: Greenburgh's diverse mix of historic homes in Edgemont, modern residences near Central Avenue, and properties surrounding the Bronx River Parkway often experience bat activity, especially in attics and eaves of older structures. The town's tree-lined neighborhoods and proximity to wooded areas make professional bat exclusion essential for protecting your investment and family's health.

faqs.question: How much does bat removal cost in Greenburgh?
faqs.answer: Bat removal and exclusion services in Greenburgh typically range from $900 to $2,500, depending on the size of the colony, number of entry points, and extent of exclusion work needed. We provide free inspections and detailed quotes before beginning any work.

faqs.question: Is bat removal legal in Greenburgh, NY?
faqs.answer: Yes, but bat removal in New York must follow strict guidelines. Bats cannot be excluded during maternity season (approximately May 1 through August 15) when flightless young are present. Our licensed technicians ensure all work complies with state and local wildlife regulations.

faqs.question: What are signs of bats in my Greenburgh home?
faqs.answer: Common signs include scratching or squeaking sounds at dusk or dawn, droppings (guano) in attics or near entry points, brown staining around openings, and spotting bats flying around your property at twilight. If you notice any of these signs, contact us immediately for an inspection.

faqs.question: How long does bat exclusion take in Greenburgh?
faqs.answer: Most bat exclusion projects in Greenburgh take 1-3 days for initial installation of one-way devices and sealing entry points. Devices typically remain in place for 3-7 days to ensure all bats have left, followed by a final sealing visit. The entire process usually takes 1-2 weeks from start to finish.
